articles.title: REDESIGN CASE STUDY 2024
articles.summary: This article goes over a complete frontend redesign of a website that involved creating a new header, a unique hero section featuring personalized hats drawn by the author's daughter, and a circular navigation for the hats section. It also incorporated interactive elements using Framer Motion and scroll-driven animations, along with the usage of CSS features like container queries, :has(), offset-path, and more.

articles.title: POSTGRES WRITE-AHEAD LOGS
articles.summary: Postgres Write-Ahead Logs (WAL) are needed for logical replication in Postgres. WAL works by storing each state change as a command in an append-only file before the change is actually made to the database, allowing for recovery from the last checkpoint in case of a crash. WAL offers various configurable parameters like `wal_level`, `fsync`, `wal_buffers`, and `checkpoint_flush_after` to optimize performance and control data retention.

articles.title: WHAT I'VE LEARNED IN THE PAST YEAR SPENT BUILDING AN AI VIDEO EDITOR
articles.summary: This developer, while building a generative video platform, used Microsoft Promptflow and Temporal to build generative workflows but encountered some challenges with unpredictable LLMs and embeddings. He found that embeddings might not be the best solution for every search problem and traditional search methods may be needed in some cases.

articles.title: MOVE FAST AND ABANDON THINGS
articles.summary: This former Mac game developer reflects on his experience of rapid prototyping and abandoning unfinished projects, arguing that this approach, while seemingly chaotic, was ultimately effective for him. He quickly tested game ideas as it allowed for the identification of truly compelling concepts. This "move fast and abandon things" philosophy was applicable even in his later career as a software engineer at Apple, where he found himself a "blue-collar" programmer amidst "white-collar" professionals.

articles.title: WHAT I TELL PEOPLE NEW TO ON-CALL
articles.summary: New on-call engineers should not fix every issue personally, but rather assess the situation and ensure it's addressed. They should also shadow experienced engineers to learn best practices and prioritize their well-being by not sacrificing personal health for non-critical issues.

articles.title: SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT TOPICS I'VE CHANGED MY MIND ON AFTER 6 YEARS IN THE INDUSTRY
articles.summary: After six years in the industry, this developer now values typed languages for team collaboration, sees standups as helpful for onboarding, and believes sprint retrospectives are valuable when focused on actual course correction. Software architecture is more important than he once thought and practicality is always better than dogmatically adhering “best practices.”

articles.title: HOW TO ADD AN ONBOARDING FLOW FOR YOUR APPLICATION WITH CLERK
articles.summary: As part of your onboarding flow, you may want to collect extra information from your user and use it to alter your application state. Follow this quick walkthrough example using Next.js and TypeScript [11] to add custom claims to your session token, read session data,

articles.title: and update with the user's onboarding state. Read the blog
UPDATED PRODUCTION-READY GEMINI MODELS, REDUCED 1.5 PRO PRICING, INCREASED RATE LIMITS, AND MORE
articles.summary: Google has released two updated production-ready Gemini models: Gemini-1.5-Pro-002 and Gemini-1.5-Flash-002. These models offer significant improvements in performance, including faster output, lower latency, and increased rate limits. The pricing for Gemini-1.5-Pro has been reduced by 50% for prompts under 128K tokens.

articles.title: ANNOUNCING TYPEDSQL: MAKE YOUR RAW SQL QUERIES TYPE-SAFE WITH PRISMA ORM
articles.summary: Prisma ORM v5.19.0 introduces TypedSQL, a new feature that brings type safety to raw SQL queries. It allows developers to use raw SQL for complex or performance-critical queries while maintaining the same type-safe environment of their workflow.

articles.title: JSON FOR YOU
articles.summary: JSON For You is a tool that visualizes and processes JSON data with features like graph and table views, comparisons, validation, nested parsing, jq support, CSV import/export, and more.

articles.title: ON IMPACTFUL AI RESEARCH
articles.summary: Impactful AI research focuses on open-source work through artifacts like models, systems, and benchmarks. Students should prioritize investing in projects over papers, selecting timely problems with large headroom and "fanout," and thinking two steps ahead while iterating fast.

articles.title: THE DATA PIPELINE IS THE NEW SECRET SAUCE
articles.summary: In AI infrastructure for companies, data pipelines are the most important part of creating and managing proprietary datasets to train and fine-tune LLMs. There are various inference hosting models to use, including hosted APIs, edge computing, on-premise data centers, and third-party cloud hosting. Generally, enterprises will start with off-the-shelf cloud solutions and progress towards specialized infrastructure for specific use cases.

articles.title: GOOGLE CACHE IS FULLY DEAD
articles.summary: Google has disabled its cache feature, which was previously used to access older versions of websites - users can now only access archived websites through the Wayback Machine.

articles.title: NANO-SPAWN
articles.summary: Nano-spawn is a tiny, dependency-free process execution library that offers a promise-based interface and various features for managing subprocesses.

articles.title: A SHORTCUT TO EDIT LONG SHELL COMMANDS IN YOUR $EDITOR
articles.summary: Using `ctrl + x` followed by `ctrl + e` in bash or adding a keybinding for `edit-command-line` in zsh allows you to edit long shell commands in your preferred editor.

articles.title: GOOGLE WINS CHALLENGE AGAINST $1.7 BILLION EU ANTITRUST FINE
articles.summary: Google won a legal challenge against a €1.49 billion ($1.66 billion) antitrust fine from the European Union.
